results.title: Alternative Pathways for Students Required to Leave School Before Completing National Certifications
results.summary: The Ministry of Education ensures students leaving school before completing national certifications have viable progression options by allowing re-admission to secondary schools if age-appropriate, offering bridging programmes, or enabling enrolment in ITE General Education for GCE "N"/"O" Levels. SkillsFuture Singapore provides subsidies for industry-relevant training, while early intervention strategies involve tracking disengaged students within schools and engaging parents, community partners, and agencies like ComLink+ for holistic support. Regarding outcome tracking, MOE monitors students while in the school system but does not track them after leaving; however, SkillsFuture Singapore and Workforce Singapore utilize broader manpower statistics to gauge upskilling indicators. The Ministry aims to combine these data points to ensure all Singaporeans have continuous pathways for upgrading throughout their careers.

results.title: Addressing Reports of Exploitation and Human Trafficking Involving Foreign Nationals from War-torn Countries in Southeast Asia
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) investigates all potential labour trafficking cases and has stepped up enforcement against companies and agencies engaging in illegal practices, such as deploying work permit holders in unauthorized occupations or submitting false declarations. MOM conducts surprise workplace checks to ensure workers are not coerced and partners with NGOs to raise awareness of employment rights through the mandatory Settling-in Programme. Migrant workers facing contraventions are encouraged to report issues to MOM; those with valid claims receive special passes to remain in Singapore and seek alternative employment. The Ministry maintains a serious stance on trafficking-in-persons while strengthening safeguards for vulnerable foreign nationals.

results.title: Adequacy of S&CC Rebates and Support Measures in Cushioning HDB Households from Pressures due to Higher Energy Costs
results.summary: The Prime Minister and Minister for Finance confirmed that existing Service and Conservancy Charges (S&CC) rebates and support measures were previously addressed in Ministerial Statements delivered on 7 April 2026 by the Deputy Prime Minister, Acting Minister for Transport, Senior Minister of State for Finance, and Coordinating Minister for National Security. These statements covered the impact of the Middle East situation on Singapore, including broader cost-of-living pressures from higher energy and fuel costs affecting HDB households. No new specific measures or updated eligibility criteria were announced during this session.

results.title: Advisories for Singaporeans Travellers Given Long Wait Times at Various US Airports
results.summary: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs does not issue advisories regarding long wait times at overseas airports, including those in the United States. Instead, the Minister advised Singaporean travellers to monitor airline and airport websites, as well as news reports, for real-time information on wait times and potential disruptions. This approach ensures travellers access specific, up-to-date data on operational constraints at their departure points rather than relying on general government alerts for airport-specific delays.

results.title: Annual Median Application Rates by Singles for Purchase of 2-room Flexi Flat from HDB for Past Six Years
results.summary: Minister Chee Hong Tat provided median application rates for first-timer singles purchasing 2-room Flexi flats in HDB Build-To-Order exercises from 2020 to 2025. The specific numerical data for each year was indicated in the response but is not included in this transcript excerpt. No further analysis, projections, or policy changes regarding these application rates were stated during the exchange. Readers seeking the exact percentage figures for each year must refer to the full official record or the accompanying data table referenced by the Minister.

results.title: Annual Number of Income-Related BTO Flats and ECs Housing Application Appeals to Ministry from 2020 to 2025
results.summary: From 2020 to 2025, the Ministry of National Development and HDB received appeals to waive income ceilings for BTO and Executive Condominium purchases by applicants exceeding monthly limits. Specific annual appeal counts for these categories were not provided in the response. The Minister noted that the Ministry and HDB are currently reviewing these income ceilings, referencing prior statements made during media interviews and the 2026 Committee of Supply debate. No further details on the review process or projected timeline for changes were stated.

results.title: Annual Skills Development Fund Disbursements for In-house On-the-job Training Subsidies Over 10 Years and Local Enterprise Support Plans
results.summary: Since 2019, the Government has disbursed close to $1 billion from the Skills Development Fund to support on-the-job training components in programmes like Career Conversion and SkillsFuture Work-Study, benefiting over 70,000 workers and 20,000 companies. Data prior to 2019 is unavailable. The Ministry confirmed it will continue expanding on-the-job training support to address enterprise needs and help workers develop industry-relevant skills. An annual breakdown of funding and beneficiaries is provided in Table 1.

results.title: Appointment of Design and Planning Consultants for Estate Upgrading Programme for Jalan Naung and Realty Park Estate
results.summary: The Building and Construction Authority called a tender in December 2025 to appoint design and planning consultants for the Estate Upgrading Programme at Jalan Naung and Realty Park Estate, but the tender remains unawarded. The Minister clarified that no plans were announced to commence physical construction by mid-2026. Instead, the project will begin with the design and planning phase, with construction scheduled to start only after this phase is completed, as confirmed in a written reply on 14 October 2025.

results.title: Approval Rates for Egg Freezing Applications Among Women Above Age 37 and Consideration to Remove Age Limit
results.summary: The Ministry of Health receives approximately 40 annual egg freezing appeals from women over 37, with about 70% approved based on individual circumstances. The authority assesses each case individually rather than using published criteria, and the Minister advised against completely removing the age limit due to declining egg quality over time. While successful pregnancy depends on multiple factors including implantation age and couple health, the current system encourages freezing eggs earlier to improve future chances. The Ministry will not fully eliminate the age restriction despite the high approval rate for older applicants.

results.title: Arrangements to Ensure Essential Supply Lines to Singapore during Regional Conflicts or Naval Disruptions
results.summary: Singapore maintains essential supply lines by diversifying sources, leveraging its status as a trusted global hub, and strengthening international partnerships. The Port of Singapore is served by a wide network of global shipping lines, while Changi Airport connects to approximately 170 cities worldwide via various airlines. This multi-operator approach reduces dependence on any single entity and ensures alternative options remain available during disruptions. Additionally, the government works with like-minded partners through bilateral and multilateral arrangements to keep trade routes open and facilitate the continued flow of essential goods during regional conflicts or naval disruptions.

results.title: Assessment Studies on Underreporting of Workplace Injuries and Effectiveness of Reporting Channels and Whistleblower Protections
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) has not conducted studies comparing workplace injury underreporting against healthcare records, as doctors are not required to maintain separate injury logs; instead, underreporting is detected via regular checks and whistleblower complaints. The average annual underreporting breaches remain low at approximately 30 cases over the past five years. Reporting channels are streamlined through the iReport system, which also initiates Work Injury Compensation insurance claims, allowing workers to report suspected unreported injuries without fear of reprisal under the Workplace Safety and Health Act. Enforcement measures were strengthened in 2020 by expanding reporting requirements to cover injuries resulting in at least one day of sick leave or light duties, and in 2024 by doubling maximum fines to $10,000 for first-time offenders and $20,000 for repeat offenders. MOM will continue monitoring trends and tightening enforcement if necessary.

results.title: Assessment of Effect on SMEs, Singapore's Energy Policy and Most-impacted Workers from War in Iran
results.summary: The Government addressed concerns regarding Middle East conflict impacts on SMEs, energy policy, and worker welfare in Ministerial Statements delivered on 7 April 2026. Specific details on assessing bunkering fuel volatility, new energy policy lessons, nuclear diversification timelines, or immediate subsidy allocations were not provided in the transcript. The Ministers did not explicitly state whether short-term cost relief is prioritized over long-term decarbonisation solutions or identify which specific sectors face the most direct impact. Consequently, the precise operational constraints, eligibility rules for relief measures, and the extent of planned cushions for affected businesses and workers remain unstated in the available record.

results.title: Assessment of Republic of Ireland's Basic Income for the Arts Scheme's Applicability towards Singapore's Arts Sector
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated that while Singapore shares Ireland's objective of supporting artists, it does not adopt a direct weekly allowance scheme due to limited fiscal resources and the need to address broader structural challenges. Instead, the government pursues a multi-dimensional ecosystem approach focused on capability development, organisational strengthening, and demand growth. Key initiatives include the 2023 Skills Framework for Arts, work-based training programmes with subsidies for self-employed trainees, and sustained funding via the Major Company Scheme and Cultural Matching Fund. To ensure long-term viability, the National Arts Council is expanding audience development through the ArtsEverywhere@CDC series, extending Arts Education Programmes to all government-supported preschools by 2027, and launching the SG Culture Pass in 2025. Additionally, new programmes targeting the one-third of the workforce comprising self-employed persons will enhance entrepreneurial capabilities and legal literacy, while the 2026 Audience Development Playbook aims to equip practitioners with skills to expand their reach.

results.title: Assessment of Threat Risk Due to the Middle East Situation and Safeguarding Domestic Stability
results.summary: The ongoing Middle East conflict has elevated global security threats, including risks of attacks, spillover incidents, and self-radicalisation via extremist narratives in cyberspace. Since October 2023, the Internal Security Department has detained or issued Restriction Orders to six Singaporeans whose radicalisation was triggered or accelerated by this conflict. The Minister stated that a vigilant and cohesive society is the best safeguard, encouraging citizens to remain alert, promptly report suspicious activities or suspected radicalisation cases, and firmly reject polarising narratives. SGSecure's "Play Your Role" campaign underscores the importance of every Singaporean's contribution to maintaining domestic stability and safety against these evolving threats.

results.title: Average Daily Non-contact Time for Educators in Preschools
results.summary: The Early Childhood Development Agency confirmed it holds no data on the average daily non-contact time for preschool educators. Staff-to-child ratios established under the 2017 Early Childhood Development Centres Act are calibrated by programme level, hours, and staff qualifications, mandating one qualified educator per eight children at the Playgroup level during programme hours. This requirement may increase to one educator per 12 children during non-programme hours or if an additional assistant educator is deployed. Preschools are permitted to exceed minimum staffing requirements to address operational needs such as leave and training, while also deploying non-certified staff to assist with routines and supervision. The Minister noted that these ratios ensure child safety and well-being but did not confirm plans for a tiered staffing model or a review of current ratios.

results.title: Average Daily Working Hours for Preschool Educators
results.summary: The Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) ceased mandatory Saturday childcare services from January 2025 following extensive stakeholder consultation involving over 8,000 parents and nearly 800 centres. While the removal of Saturday shifts aims to improve educator well-being, some operators have slightly increased daily working hours post-shift to accommodate lesson preparation and classroom setup, whereas others maintain existing hours. ECDA noted that educator working hours remain contractually determined. To address sector concerns and enhance long-term manpower resilience, ECDA will convene a sector-wide review in the second quarter of 2026 to examine operating processes and support structures. This initiative seeks to improve the overall value proposition of an early childhood career and optimize educators' daily work experience without altering the current five-day work week framework.

results.title: Average Duration between Issuance of HDB Flat Eligibility Letter and Signing of Lease Agreement
results.summary: Introduced in May 2023, the HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter provides upfront clarity on eligibility, grants, and loan amounts, with validity extended from six to nine months in November 2023. Buyers expiring within 30 days may apply for a fresh letter, typically processed within seven working days, while existing letters remain valid for application purposes until a new one is issued. Upon successful application, buyers are invited to book a flat and subsequently sign the Agreement for Lease, typically within nine months after booking. Notably, the HDB housing loan amount remains unchanged even if the HFE letter expires by the time of booking. For those with genuine needs to review their loan amount due to income changes, HDB offers case-by-case assistance rather than a systematic update mechanism. The government has no plans to further extend the validity period to ensure the currency of submitted information.

results.title: Breakdown of Closed Cases of COMIT and CREST Programmes by Sessions and Proportion of Drop-out Cases
results.summary: In 2024, the Community Intervention Team (COMIT) and Community Resource, Engagement and Support Team (CREST) managed over 15,000 and 18,000 cases respectively, with 46% and 36% discharged or discontinued services. Session distribution for closed cases is detailed in Table 1. The Ministry of Health does not track the specific number of drop-outs or reasons for service discontinuation. Consequently, the proportion of cases classified strictly as drop-outs remains untracked and unavailable.

results.title: Breakdown of Data on Senior Management Roles by Citizenship and Employment Status in MNCs Across Sectors
results.summary: In 2025, Singapore Citizens, Permanent Residents, and Employment Pass holders held 67.3%, 17.4%, and 15.3% of senior management roles in Multinational Corporations (MNCs) respectively. These roles are concentrated in the services sector, specifically within higher-skilled industries like financial services, information and communications, and professional services, where Citizens and PRs form the majority. The Ministry for Manpower provided this specific breakdown without indicating plans to track future data differently.

results.title: Breakdown of Outrage of Modesty Reports by Perpetrators' Categories
results.summary: In 2025, over half of reported outrage of modesty cases involved perpetrators known to the victims. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs provided a breakdown of these known perpetrators, noting that friends accounted for 20%, relatives for 10%, colleagues or supervisors for 11%, and teachers for 2%. The remaining cases involved unknown perpetrators. These statistics were presented during a parliamentary question session regarding the categorization of offenders in modesty reports.

results.title: Clarification by Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs
results.summary: Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs Mr Shanmugam clarified data regarding traffic accidents linked to substance use between 2023 and 2025. The corrected record states that 38 accidents involved suspected drug and/or etomidate use, comprising 19 drug cases, 12 etomidate cases, and seven cases involving both substances. This revision adjusts the previously reported figures of 77 total suspected cases and 25 drug-only cases. The clarification addresses a written answer provided on 3 February 2026 in response to a Parliamentary Question filed by Ms Valerie Lee.

results.title: Clarification by Minister of State for National Development
results.summary: Part 4 of the Bill introduces offences and penalties to safeguard against unlawful animal practice and misrepresentation by unauthorised persons. The maximum financial penalty for such violations will be raised to $50,000, representing a $40,000 increase from the current $10,000 level under the Animal and Bird Act. This adjustment strengthens deterrence against profit-driven motives and aligns penalties with those applicable in human healthcare professions. The Minister of State for National Development issued this factual correction regarding the penalty increase amount during the Sitting of 8 April 2026.

results.title: Clarification by Senior Parliamentary Secretary to the Minister for Manpower
results.summary: Senior Parliamentary Secretary to the Minister for Manpower clarified that Career Conversion Programmes provide salary support to employers. This measure is designed to assist companies in hiring and training new employees by offsetting their salary costs. The clarification addresses Parliamentary Question No 16 regarding the specific financial assistance mechanism available under this initiative. No further details on eligibility criteria or application timelines were provided in this statement.

results.title: Comparison of University Graduate Employment Numbers Among Survey Methodologies and Assessment of Decline in Graduate Employment
results.summary: The Joint Autonomous Universities Graduate Employment Survey (JAUGES) methodology remains unchanged, though JAUGES 2025 introduces a new "secured employment" category to capture job offers and entrepreneurial steps alongside traditional employment. All prior indicators, including the employment rate, continue to be reported. For the assessment of the declining employment trend, the Minister directed members to refer to the Ministry of Education's combined reply to Oral Questions 105 and 106 issued on 7 April 2026, rather than providing a direct analysis within this response.

results.title: Complaints Received on Defective Non-vehicle Consumer Goods Under Consumer Protection Act and Outcomes of Complaints from 2021 to 2025
results.summary: From 2021 to 2025, the Consumers Association of Singapore received between 156 and 176 annual complaints regarding defective non-vehicle consumer goods under the Consumer Protection (Fair Trading) Act. Over two-thirds were resolved in the consumer's favour, while approximately 26% of unresolved cases stemmed from failed amicable negotiations or lack of response. The remaining 7% involve ongoing negotiations. The three primary reasons for unsuccessful resolutions include disputes over defect status versus wear-and-tear, disagreements on return policies such as refunds versus repairs, and insufficient partial refund offers from suppliers.

results.title: Consideration for Extension of HDB Flat Eligibility Letter Validity Period for Unsuccessful BTO Applicant Re-Applications
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that HDB has no plans to further extend the validity period of the Housing and Development Board (HDB) Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter beyond its current nine-month duration, as a longer period risks rendering eligibility assessments less current. Introduced in May 2023 and extended in November 2023, the letter provides upfront clarity on grant and loan amounts, with applications for fresh letters processed within seven working days if the existing letter expires within 30 days. Buyers whose letters expire after successful application booking retain their eligible housing loan amounts, and those requiring a review of loan eligibility can seek case-by-case assistance from HDB.

results.title: Considerations for Permanent Member Appointments to Presidential Council for Minority Rights and Misconduct Removal Procedures
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth explained that permanent members of the Presidential Council for Minority Rights (PCMR) are individuals of distinction appointed for life, while non-permanent members serve three-year terms. Under Article 73 of the Constitution, all members must vacate their seats if they cease to be Singapore citizens, resign, or become disqualified under Article 72. Specific disqualifications include having unsound mind, being an undischarged bankrupt, or being convicted of an offence punishable by at least one year's imprisonment or a fine of at least $10,000. The Minister did not elaborate on additional mechanisms for misconduct removal beyond these statutory disqualifications.

results.title: Considerations in Determining Setback Distance and Other Safety Requirements for Energy Storage Systems in Fire Code
results.summary: The Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) developed Fire Code requirements for Energy Storage Systems (ESS) by collaborating with international experts and referencing standards like NFPA 855. The Code stipulates specific safety measures, including minimum setback distances, fire-rated compartmentation, maximum storage limits, and enhanced sprinkler systems. SCDF has already implemented differentiated fire safety requirements for ESS installations based on their specific fire risk profiles, which are determined by factors such as potential for fire spread, explosion, and toxic gas emissions. The Minister confirmed these differentiated requirements are currently in place.

results.title: Custodial Parent Rights to Restrict Non-custodial Parent Access to Child's Personal and Medical Records
results.summary: Sole custodial parents may request relevant agencies to restrict a non-custodial parent from modifying a child's personal records, subject to case-by-case assessment based on documentation and agency policy. The Minister noted that children benefit from effective co-parenting and communication. If disputes persist regarding the ability to restrict record modifications, the sole custodial parent may apply to the Court for resolution. No specific timeline or statutory framework for such requests was detailed in the response.

results.title: Data on Parental Consent Cited as Barrier to Accessing Mental Health Services for Vulnerable Minors
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) does not actively track data on minors citing parental consent as a barrier to accessing mental health services in 2024 and 2025. The Minister noted that this barrier likely affects households across all income levels, including those in out-of-home care or experiencing family violence, particularly when parents contribute to the minor's distress. MOH is currently developing guidelines to address this issue, though no specific timeline or comparative statistics were provided. Consequently, the requested quantitative data and detailed breakdowns remain unavailable.

results.title: Data on Victim and Perpetrator Profiles in Rape and Molest Cases
results.summary: From 2024 to 2025, reported cases of outrage of modesty rose from 1,427 to 1,531, while rape cases increased from 401 to 479. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs confirmed that the majority of rape cases and over half of outrage of modesty cases involve offenders known to the victims, with these proportions remaining stable over the last five years. In 2025, approximately 33% of total cases occurred in residential premises, 18% in commercial premises, and 3% in educational institutions. The Government maintains a firm stance against all sexual offending and has implemented measures across settings, including teaching students to report concerns in schools and deploying specialist agencies like Protection Specialist Centres to provide support for victims of intra- and extra-familial violence. Police also continue to provide victim care services during investigations.

results.title: Developing Working Definitions of Emerging Work Arrangements for Policymaking and Plans to Collect Data on Fractional Work Arrangements in Future Labour Force Surveys
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower and tripartite partners are currently studying fractional work arrangements under the Tripartite Workgroup on Senior Employment (TWG-SE) to support evolving economic conditions and work preferences. As these arrangements remain in flux without an established international statistical definition, current data on part-time workers and multiple job holders partially capture related trends. The Ministry will explore better definitions and data collection methods as the concept develops, with findings from the TWG-SE scheduled for release later this year.

results.title: Drawing on Fuel Equalisation Fund to Buffer PTOs Against Rising Energy Costs
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that rail operators no longer require the Fuel Equalisation Fund due to a new energy index in the fare adjustment formula, which tracks year-on-year diesel and electricity price changes. For bus operators, service fees are already indexed to energy prices. During the 2025 Fare Review Exercise, the Government provided over $200 million in subsidies to cover costs associated with deferring maximum allowable fare increases, ensuring adjustments remain manageable. Additionally, the Government provides more than $2 billion annually in subsidies to sustain bus and train services. The Public Transport Council balances fare affordability with operator financial sustainability while exercising discretion to defer fare adjustments when necessary.

results.title: Drivers Caught Watching Videos or Using Auto-scrolling Social Media Applications whilst Driving
results.summary: The Traffic Police does not collect or track data regarding drivers caught watching videos or using auto-scrolling social media applications while driving. Consequently, the Ministry cannot confirm whether there is an increasing trend in such incidents. This lack of specific data collection means no statistics or trend analysis are available for this particular enforcement category.

results.title: Due Diligence Checks by Banks and Safeguards Such As Deduction Limits for GIRO Transactions
results.summary: The Minister of State confirmed that existing GIRO safeguards include customer-set transaction limits and bank due diligence checks on billing organisations, though these may not prevent all errors or misuse. MAS is currently reviewing the framework to strengthen consumer protections, considering measures such as enabling monthly limits on transaction values and counts, enhancing transaction monitoring, and potentially incorporating features like advance payment notices. The review will also examine suggestions for optional limits on deduction frequency and cumulative amounts per billing period. While some banks already offer on-demand GIRO requiring individual authorisation, major system enhancements requiring development and testing will take time to implement. MAS will collaborate with banks to raise public awareness of current protections and limitations during the review process.

results.title: Effect of Closures of Small F&B Shops on Neighbourhood Vibrancy, Social Cohesion and Community Resilience
results.summary: HDB rental shop vacancy rates have remained low and stable over the past five years. The agency considers some business churn healthy, enabling less competitive operators to exit while new businesses enter to better serve resident needs. To maintain variety, HDB employs trade mix controls and assesses trade designations for vacant units before tendering them out as soon as possible. This process ensures a diverse range of products and services are available to the community.

results.title: Effectiveness of Noise-monitoring Meter Pilot at Clementi
results.summary: The Holland-Bukit Panjang Town Council installed a noise-monitoring meter at Clementi Community Hall in December as a pilot to help activity organisers maintain appropriate sound levels. The Ministry and HDB have not yet assessed the pilot's effectiveness or determined criteria for wider rollout in dense HDB precincts. The Town Council will require time to evaluate the solution's suitability before deciding on expansion. Any useful learning points from this pilot will be shared with other Town Councils and Government agencies to assist in managing noise issues within dense residential environments.

results.title: Employment Reference System Transparency Reform and Worker Rights Verification Processes for Migrant Domestic Workers
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) maintains that prospective employers and migrant domestic workers (MDWs) have different hiring priorities, so the Ministry does not require employers to submit feedback directly to MOM. Instead, past employers act as referees by providing contact details for prospective employers to reach out directly, with any feedback shared between employers without MOM verification or attachment to the worker's employment record. From 2021 to 2025, instances where past employers offered to act as referees accounted for less than 2% of MDWs whose work permits expired or were cancelled annually. Neither MDWs nor prospective employers are notified when a past employer offers to be a referee, and MOM has no plans to change this existing process or provide workers with access to such feedback.

results.title: Encouraging Adoption of Battery Energy Storage Systems Over Diesel Generators
results.summary: The Energy Market Authority facilitates Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) adoption for grid resilience to support intermittent renewables, citing a 2023 utility-scale deployment by Sembcorp. Diesel generators remain the primary solution for off-grid applications like night markets and continuous backup power where fuel is available. End-users retain the autonomy to select between BESS and diesel generators based on specific operational requirements. Market forces determine fees for Qualified Persons and Registered Inspectors, while the Singapore Civil Defence Force and EMA continue to enforce safety standards and technical requirements for BESS deployment without new regulatory interventions on inspection costs.

results.title: Energy Security, Supply Resilience and Business Support Measures Against Middle East Disruptions and Price Volatility
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry addressed questions regarding energy security and business support in a Ministerial Statement delivered on 7 April 2026. The Government confirmed that the establishment of Singapore GasCo Pte Ltd aims to strengthen resilience against potential natural gas and LNG supply disruptions stemming from the Middle East conflict. While specific contingency arrangements for electricity generation and price stability were referenced, detailed operational mechanisms were not elaborated in the provided transcript. Furthermore, the Ministerial Statement covered inquiries on petrol price pass-through assessments, fuel stock sufficiency under Strait of Hormuz disruption scenarios, and considerations for time-limited conditional support for businesses facing unanticipated energy cost increases. The response noted that further measures to address identified gaps are under consideration but did not specify exact timelines or eligibility criteria for the proposed support schemes.

results.title: Enforcement Actions against Retention and Usage of Deregistered Vehicles and Cases with Links to Organised Syndicates for Past Three Years
results.summary: Over the past three years, approximately 2,500 annual violations regarding the illegal retention or use of deregistered vehicles have occurred, with prosecutions typically commenced or concluded. During this period, 415 vehicles were seized. The Acting Minister for Transport noted that roughly 43% of these cases involved repeat offenders. The transcript does not specify the exact number of cases linked to organised syndicates, nor does it provide a precise count of prosecutions concluded versus those still pending.

results.title: Enhancing Suicide Reporting Guidance and Safeguards Amid Rise in Imitative Behaviours with Social Media Algorithmic Amplification
results.summary: The Minister for Digital Development and Information confirmed that mainstream media generally reports suicide cases sensitively, while the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) Code of Practice mandates designated social media services to implement systems minimizing exposure to harmful content, including suicide and self-harm material. Under the Social Media Services Code, platforms must actively offer local suicide prevention hotlines to users searching high-risk terms and may be directed by IMDA to disable access to content advocating or instructing on suicide. The Ministry will continue engaging with media to ensure responsible reporting and will monitor and update efforts as relevant. The response did not specify new legislative measures or a timeline for updating the Code of Practice beyond ongoing monitoring.

results.title: Enhancing Support Ecosystem for Migrant Domestic Workers
results.summary: The 2024 Institute of Mental Health and NUS study indicates elevated depressive and anxiety symptoms among migrant domestic workers (MDWs) compared to other migrant workers, though findings reflect self-reported responses rather than clinical diagnoses. To address this, the Ministry of Manpower maintains a multi-pronged support ecosystem including the Settling-In Programme, Centre for Domestic Employees interviews, mandatory welfare checks within three months, and six-monthly medical examinations. MOM will strengthen NGO partnerships to enhance early detection, specifically training volunteer "care sisters" via the Alliance of Domestic Employees Outreach and amplifying assistance through the CDE and Brahm Centre. A dedicated MDW helpline and WhatsApp channel remain available for immediate support, with plans to expand these channels to ensure timely mental health screening and community-based intervention for the migrant workforce.

results.title: Extension of Asset-conversion Framework for Funding of SERS Land Acquisitions to VERS
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that extending the asset-conversion framework from the Selective En bloc Redevelopment Scheme (SERS) to the Voluntary Early Redevelopment Scheme (VERS) is not currently under discussion. He explained that VERS is a long-term, complex undertaking where the policy framework, including funding sources, is still being designed. Consequently, it is too early to determine if VERS acquisitions will be value-neutral or funded by Past Reserves. The President's Office consultation status was not addressed as details remain part of the ongoing policy design process.

results.title: Fatal and Major Injuries Suffered by Platform Workers, and Proposal to Review Platform Incentive Structures
results.summary: In 2025, platform workers recorded two fatalities and 74 major injuries, with 62 major injuries involving delivery services and 12 involving ride-hail services. While road safety is a primary concern, the Ministry lacks data linking injuries to work pressures or fatigue, though associations note customer timing expectations and earnings anxiety may contribute to rushing. The Ministry is not reviewing incentive structures directly but supports industry-wide principles developed by the Platform Work Trilateral Group to prevent schemes that encourage excessive work without rest. To strengthen safeguards, the Ministry of Manpower convened a Platform Work Safety Workgroup in March 2026, focusing on two-wheeled vehicle safety and collective risk mitigation. The group will share findings and recommendations once ready, balancing safety improvements with the need to avoid stifling innovation or disrupting business models.

results.title: Energy Conservation (Amendment) Bill
results.summary: The Energy Conservation (Amendment) Bill seeks to extend Minimum Energy Performance Standards (MEPS) and Mandatory Energy Labelling Scheme (MELS) coverage to goods imported directly by individuals or businesses for personal use, addressing concerns over affordability and digital marketplace transparency. While specific queries regarding Clause 7 registration fees ($70–$85) and Clause 12 free sample requirements remain under consideration, the Bill aims to ensure a level playing field between local and imported products. Regarding hypothetical scenarios where software updates degrade hardware energy performance, the National Environment Agency (NEA) and Director-General retain enforcement powers to issue notices for rectification, warnings, or fines depending on whether the issue is rectifiable. Future models from manufacturers engaging in such practices will face heightened scrutiny during review, though no retrospective revocation of existing registrations or mandatory cessation of usage is currently mandated for compliant goods.

results.title: Fire Safety Requirements, Protocols and Enforcement Measures for Hawker Centres and Food Establishments
results.summary: Under the Fire Safety Act, public buildings with an occupant load exceeding 200 persons, including hawker centres, must establish emergency response plans and conduct annual evacuation drills. The Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF), partnering with the National Environment Agency and the National Fire and Emergency Preparedness Council, regularly educates stallholders on fire prevention and response through community outreach programmes. Cooking with liquefied petroleum gas, kitchen exhaust duct maintenance, and fire safety provisions like hose reels are regulated under the Fire Code. SCDF enforces these requirements via scheduled visits and unannounced inspections triggered by public feedback. Premise owners or occupiers breaching fire safety rules, such as failing to maintain kitchen exhaust ducts, face fines up to $10,000 and/or imprisonment for up to six months.

results.title: Flexibility Extended to Home Buyers Affected by Earlier-than-expected BTO Completion
results.summary: In 2025, approximately 30% of Build-To-Order (BTO) blocks were completed about two months earlier than estimated, prompting HDB to notify buyers at one year, six months, and three months prior to expected completion. While buyers must collect keys and pay the balance within a month of readiness, those facing genuine difficulties can request assistance. HDB may grant key collection deferrals to allow additional time for selling existing flats or arranging financing. For existing flat owners requiring back-to-back sales, HDB provides updates at three junctures to facilitate planning; however, if financial difficulties prevent matching standard back-to-back arrangements, HDB will intervene to facilitate acceptable solutions for both buyers and sellers.

results.title: Flexibility for Proximity or Priority Scheme Appeals for BTO or Resale Flats where Distance Requirement is Slightly Exceeded for Caregiving or Valid Reasons
results.summary: The Minister stated that the four-kilometre radius for the Family Care Scheme (FCS) and Proximity Housing Grant (PHG) remains adequate to meet buyers' needs. This distance requirement was relaxed in 2018 from "within two kilometres or the same town" to allow access to a wider range of housing options. No flexibility will be granted to applicants who fall slightly outside this four-kilometre radius, even if they are primary caregivers for their parents. The existing framework is considered sufficient for mutual care and support between parents and children.

results.title: Food Wastage and Student Satisfaction Rates in Schools with Central Kitchen Meal Model
results.summary: The Minister addressed food wastage and student satisfaction under the Central Kitchen Meal Model (CKMM), noting initial waste among primary students due to adjustment to pre-ordered, balanced meals lacking on-the-spot customization. Operators have responded to feedback by substituting unpopular items like celery with broccoli and expanding daily menu choices from a few stalls to six to 14 options with weekly or daily rotation. While MOE is establishing clear satisfaction benchmarks and tracking popularity data to fine-tune menus against Health Promotion Board nutritional standards, the Minister clarified that satisfying all varied student tastes is not possible. The Ministry remains committed to improving the model through continuous collaboration with schools and operators to ensure access to nutritious, affordable, and appealing food, while also exploring complementary canteen models.

results.title: Guidelines Ensuring Safety and Well-being of Auxiliary Police Officers While Managing Traffic at Road Junctions
results.summary: The Singapore Police Force issues directives to Auxiliary Police Forces (APFs) ensuring officer safety and well-being during traffic management duties. Key measures include limiting deployment hours and mandating sufficient rest periods between shifts. These operational requirements address manpower constraints while maintaining service continuity and reliability. The directives also cover operational competency and equipping standards for Auxiliary Police Officers (APOs). Specific guidelines for road junction deployments are part of these broader safety protocols.

results.title: Haj Arrangements and Contingency Plans for Singapore Pilgrims Given Present Security Situation and Travel Disruptions in Middle East
results.summary: Preparations for the 2026 Hajj are proceeding as planned, with MUIS coordinating direct flights and ground transport to mitigate Middle East disruptions. Should arrangements be cancelled or significantly disrupted, affected pilgrims will receive priority for subsequent years, subject to Saudi Arabia's annual quota. MUIS is collaborating with AMTAS and service providers to explore financial assistance, including potential refunds and deferral options, depending on prevailing circumstances. The agency will continue monitoring developments and will keep pilgrims informed of support measures and latest arrangements in a timely manner to ensure safety and welfare remain the utmost priority.

results.title: Help for Students Registering for Primary School when Schools Nearest Their Homes Do Not Offer Their Mother Tongue Language
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the Ministry plans schools to ensure students access a primary school near their homes. With the exception of 15 Special Assistance Plan schools, all primary schools offer the three official mother tongue languages, including Tamil and Malay. Consequently, children from these language communities are not disadvantaged under the Primary 1 registration framework as they can register at a nearby school offering their mother tongue. The Minister did not provide specific timelines or additional eligibility conditions for this arrangement.

results.title: Helping NSFs with Mental Conditions Link Up with External Health and Social Sector Partners for Better Care
results.summary: The SAF currently supports Full-time National Servicemen (NSFs) with psychosocial stressors through internal systems and collaborations with Ministry of Social and Family Development partners like Social Service Offices and Family Service Centres to ensure post-service support continuity. A pilot programme called Case Connect is being tested to facilitate direct joint case management between SAF medical officers and social service officers. The Ministry of Defence will study integrating better communication with the public healthcare system to address identified gaps. Regarding commander training, while unit commanders are trained on support schemes, the Minister noted that specific education on referring NSFs to external resources is not currently in the curriculum but will be reviewed to improve efficiency. NSFs requiring assistance are encouraged to contact their commanders, the SAF Mental Health Office, or the counselling hotline.

results.title: IVF Usage Rates through EEF Programme since June 2023 and Consideration to Remove Marriage Requirement for Use of Frozen Eggs
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not track statistics on women who froze eggs under the Elective Egg Freezing (EEF) programme and subsequently used them for in-vitro fertilisation (IVF) in Singapore or abroad. Consequently, no usage trends or data regarding Singaporean women undergoing IVF overseas since June 2023 are available. Regarding the marriage requirement for IVF, the Ministry maintains that this rule aligns with the Government's existing position based on current social norms defining marriage as a union between a man and a woman. The Ministry has not indicated plans to remove this requirement.

results.title: Impact Assessment of Rising Energy and Food Prices on Varying Household Groups and Targeted Support Measures
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry addressed the impact of rising energy and food prices on household groups in a Ministerial Statement delivered on 7 April 2026. This statement, jointly issued by the Acting Minister for Transport, Senior Minister of State for Finance, and Coordinating Minister for National Security, covered the distributional effects of the Middle East conflict and corresponding support measures. The specific details regarding current assessments, potential future impacts, and the sufficiency of targeted support were provided during that parliamentary sitting rather than in this specific exchange.

results.title: Impact Assessment on Existing Government Payout Recipients Facing Cost of Living Challenges and Support Plans
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that the Ministry's initiatives prioritize enhancing employability and income through upskilling and training across all career stages. Lower-wage workers facing cost-of-living pressures are supported via the Workfare Income Supplement, which targets the bottom 20% of Singaporean workers. Additionally, the Progressive Wage Model, covering approximately nine in ten full-time lower-wage resident workers, aims to uplift wages by upgrading skills and improving productivity. The Minister did not provide specific data on the proportion of existing payout recipients unable to sustain current living costs.

results.title: Impact of Economic Anxieties on Social Insularity
results.summary: The 2026 Edelman Trust Barometer identifies economic anxieties, including job security and cost-of-living pressures, as drivers of rising social insularity, though the Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth (MCCY) lacks a local study confirming this specific relationship. To address these concerns, the Government is refreshing its social compact through the Economic Strategy Review to drive growth and create jobs, while providing broad-based financial support via the Cost-of-Living Special Payment, U-Save rebates, and CDC vouchers. Operational measures include initiatives like Jobs Nearby @ CDC to aid job searches and ArtsEverywhere @ CDC to activate communities. MCCY collaborates with public agencies and private sector stakeholders to strengthen shared values through interfaith dialogues, racial harmony efforts, and newcomer integration programmes. The strategy also expands common spaces for interaction, such as inter-school sports events, and enhances youth pathways through mentoring and development programmes for artists and athletes. Additionally, the Community Foundation of Singapore's Collective for a Stronger Society offers holistic support in education, employment, health, and social assistance to lower-income families

results.title: Impact of MNC Regional Headquarters Relocation on Youth Job Opportunities and Corporate Retention Strategies
results.summary: The number of foreign headquarters in Singapore increased by 33.8% from 770 in 2019 to 1,030 in 2024, while resident professionals employed in these HQs rose from 12,700 to 13,700 over the same period. The Government attributes MNC retention to strengthening Singapore's value proposition, including good governance, rule of law, a well-trained workforce, and strong science and technology capabilities. The Minister stated that data on the specific reasons for Singaporean youths relocating overseas for full-time employment is not collected, meaning no statistics exist regarding job losses linked to MNC relocations.

results.title: Impact of Traffic Data Distinction between Local and Foreign Vehicles in LTA's Calibration Model for ERP Rates
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that LTA's ERP calibration model does not distinguish between local and foreign-registered vehicles when determining rates. Currently, foreign vehicles with an In-Vehicle Unit (IU) pay standard ERP charges, while those without an IU pay a $5 daily flat fee. Although the model does not separately account for the proportion of traffic lacking marginal price sensitivity, the 2024 ratio of foreign vehicles with IUs to those paying the flat fee is 4:3. This distinction remains unincorporated into the rate-setting process despite the existing fee structure differences.

results.title: Impact of War in Iran on Job Security and Retrenchment Risk in Sectors with Significant Fuel and Freight Cost Exposure
results.summary: Refer to the Ministerial Statement delivered by Deputy Prime Minister Gan on 7 April 2026 for the Ministry's assessment regarding the impact of the Iran war and fuel price surges. This statement addresses job security and retrenchment risks specifically within sectors facing significant fuel and freight cost exposure, including aviation, maritime and port operations, land transport, wholesale trade, and logistics. The Minister directed members to this existing official record rather than providing new details during the current sitting. No additional operational constraints or timelines were stated in this exchange.

results.title: Improving Motor Vehicle Ownership Transfer Process
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that LTA's motor vehicle ownership transfer process already mandates SingPass authentication for both parties and requires two-step confirmation before completion. Existing advisories on the OneMotoring website explicitly urge sellers to transfer ownership only after receiving payment. Additionally, a CaseTrust-Singapore Vehicle Traders Association accreditation scheme assesses second-hand dealers for fair practices, transparency, and dispute resolution capabilities. The Minister noted that these existing safeguards and the option to engage accredited dealers are currently in place to enhance consumer protection without introducing new licensing requirements for all dealers.

results.title: Inclusion of DBSS Flats in Goodwill Repair Assistance Scheme
results.summary: The Goodwill Repair Assistance (GRA) scheme does not extend to Design, Build and Sell Scheme (DBSS) flats, as these were developed by private developers rather than HDB. Consequently, GRA eligibility is restricted to HDB-developed flats only. For DBSS units, private developers provided a one-year Defects Liability Period and extended warranties covering ceiling leaks (five years) and spalling concrete (ten years). After these periods expire, owners of affected upper and lower floor units share responsibility for repairing wear-and-tear ceiling leaks. HDB offers technical guidance and a list of contractors for repairs but does not provide financial assistance for DBSS flats beyond the warranty terms.

results.title: Inclusion of Gender-specific Considerations in Guidelines on Parental Consent for Minor Mental Health Services in View of Higher Rates of Anxiety and Depression Among Adolescent Girls
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health stated that the expert group developing guidelines on parental consent for minor mental health services aims to balance timely access with parental involvement and safety safeguards. The guidelines are designed to guide care professionals in providing services to young persons regardless of gender or specific mental health symptoms. While the Minister acknowledged the question regarding gender differences in help-seeking behaviour and higher rates of anxiety and depression among adolescent girls, the response did not specify how these factors will be explicitly incorporated into the final guidelines or provide a timeline for their release.

results.title: Industry Skills Council's Involvement in Development of Current Skills Framework and Practitioner Engagement to Ensure Alignment with Operational Skills
results.summary: SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) employs a data-driven approach to develop skills frameworks across 38 sectors, utilizing labour market surveillance data like job postings to identify in-demand skills. The agency consulted employers, industry associations, unions, training organisations, and Government agencies to refine these frameworks, which are continually refreshed. Higher learning institutes collaborate with industry partners to integrate practical exposure, such as work attachments and internships, into curricula. This ensures training remains responsive to operational skills and industry needs.

results.title: Infant Food Safety Testing for Imported Infant Milk Powder
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency (SFA) tests an average of eight infant formula products monthly for contaminants, with no recalls recorded between 2019 and 2025. Prior to January 2026, cereulide testing was not included as it was considered uncommon and lacked Codex Alimentarius standards. Following global recalls triggered by cereulide detection in January 2026, SFA stepped up market surveillance at major retail outlets, initiated specific cereulide testing, and recalled affected products. The agency will continue monitoring developments and adjusting its sampling and testing strategy to ensure product safety.

results.title: Informal Target Quotas of and Allocation of Placement for Singaporeans in PhD Programmes in Autonomous Universities
results.summary: There are no informal or target quotas for Singaporean PhD places in autonomous universities (AUs), though the Government collaborates with AUs to build a strong local academic talent base. The Singapore Teaching and Academic Research Talent (START) scheme offers scholarships and awards to support Singaporeans pursuing academic careers. Singaporean PhD students receive the highest tuition fee subsidies and stipends reflecting their citizenship status. Currently, Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents constitute approximately one-third of the total PhD student population at AUs. The Ministry did not commit to encouraging AUs to consider specific quotas beyond existing support mechanisms.

results.title: Integration of Court Custody Orders with Healthcare, School and Residential Records Systems
results.summary: The Minister for Law stated that automatic dissemination of sensitive custody orders across healthcare, school, and residential platforms is not currently implemented due to privacy risks. Parents retain the discretion to inform relevant agencies of custody, care, and control orders. The government remains open to feedback regarding scenarios where the benefits of sharing such information across different settings outweigh the risks. No specific timeline for developing a cross-agency system was provided, and the matter requires careful handling of private data.

results.title: Investigation on DBS and POSB Banking Services Outage and Strengthening Digital Banking Resilience
results.summary: On 19 March 2026, DBS experienced a one-hour digital banking outage (12:03 pm to 1:19 pm) caused by an erroneous system change step, preventing access to deposit balances and some payment channels while ATMs and cards remained functional. MAS expects banks to limit unscheduled downtime for critical systems to four hours within any rolling 12-month period. Following the incident, MAS will follow up with DBS to strengthen its change management process. The authority has clarified that while operational disruptions can occur due to system complexity, swift and safe recovery is mandatory. MAS will continue monitoring banks to ensure they meet high standards of system resiliency and reliability for digital banking services.

results.title: Investigations of Singapore-based Companies in 19 March 2026 US Indictment on Unlawful Diversion of AI Technology to China
results.summary: The US has not shared information regarding the 19 March 2026 indictment on unlawful AI technology diversion to China with the Singapore Government. Consequently, no investigation into Singapore-incorporated companies or persons for breaches of US export controls is currently underway. The Government enforces only its own laws and does not enforce foreign export controls, though it expects all local companies to consider such regulations during international business. Businesses deliberately using Singapore to circumvent other nations' export controls are not condoned.

results.title: Job Assistance Requests from Singaporean Fresh Graduates in 2025
results.summary: In 2025, Workforce Singapore (WSG) and the Employment and Employability Institute (e2i) received close to 2,000 job assistance requests from unemployed Singaporean university graduates. Separately, approximately 1,500 recent graduates assisted by these agencies secured employment during the year. The Minister noted that this placement figure includes individuals who sought assistance in earlier years due to the typical time lag between applying for help and securing a job. The government does not track or report the specific origin of these graduates regarding whether they attended local or overseas universities.

results.title: Key Factors for Strong Labour Market Demand for Engineers and Technology Specialists, While PMETs Face Retrenchments
results.summary: Labour market mismatches stem from shifting skill requirements, where firms expand technology roles like AI engineering while automating other PMET functions. As of December 2025, finance and infocomm sectors held 4,100 and 5,300 PMET vacancies respectively, contrasting with only 960 retrenchments in Q4 2025, indicating ample opportunities. The Government supports reskilling through Workforce Singapore's Career Conversion Programmes, which have placed over 7,300 workers into tech and engineering roles over three years via employer salary subsidies. WSG collaborates with higher learning institutions and professional societies to ensure course relevance to industry needs, addressing concerns about designing programmes directly aligned with specific professional sectors rather than relying solely on academic institutions.

results.title: LTA Data Metrics to Determine EV Charging Point Adequacy in Bidadari Estate's HDB Carparks
results.summary: LTA monitors EV charger utilisation, noting Bidadari's slow chargers operate at 40% capacity versus 20% nationwide. Additional slow chargers will be deployed in Bidadari this year, contingent on electrical capacity. While infrastructure upgrades are ongoing to enable a future fast-charging hub, immediate alternatives include existing fast chargers at Woodleigh Mall and nearby petrol kiosks. The Authority will consider adding a fast-charging hub in Bidadari once electrical constraints are resolved.

results.title: LTA Staff Deployment Pilot Programme for Peak Hour Commuter Etiquette Interventions at Busy MRT Stations
results.summary: Public Transport Operators currently deploy staff at peak hours in busier stations to manage crowds and encourage courteous behaviour, such as yielding to alighting passengers and wheelchair users. The Ministry announced the introduction of more prominent priority queues for boarding trains and lifts to nudge commuters to give way to those in need. Additionally, the Public Transport Council and Land Transport Authority will continue public education efforts using the "Thoughtful Bunch" characters "Move-In Martin" and "Bag-Down Benny" to promote gracious commuter behaviour. No specific pilot programme for active behavioural nudges at Hougang MRT station was confirmed beyond existing measures.

results.title: Listing of Foreign Organisations That May Pose Security Concerns and Advisory for Citizens Volunteering Overseas
results.summary: The Government will not publish a broader list of foreign organisations posing national security concerns, citing operational and security risks, though existing lists under the Terrorism (Suppression of Financing) Act and restrictions under the Foreign Interference (Countermeasures) Act and United Nations Act remain in force. No centralised point of contact or new formal guidance channels for overseas volunteering will be established; instead, the Government relies on existing statutes, travel advisories, and the SGSecure national movement to disseminate security information. While no new advisories or requirements for volunteers are being introduced, the Minister emphasised that individuals bear ultimate responsibility to exercise due diligence, avoid foreign armed conflicts, and comply with laws regarding foreign militaries, intelligence agencies, and political organisations. Awareness efforts focus on amplifying existing messages through media and inter-agency outreach rather than creating new partnerships or upstream school programmes.

results.title: Main Channels of Reports for Outrage of Modesty Cases
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs stated that the Police do not track the specific breakdown of reporting channels, such as in-person visits, online submissions, or hotline calls, for outrage of modesty cases. This lack of data exists because multiple reports via different channels may be filed for a single incident. Consequently, the Police follow up on all reports promptly and thoroughly regardless of the submission method. No specific statistics on channel usage or case volume for 2025 were provided in the response.

results.title: Mandatory Government Security Vetting for Personnel with Access to Singapore’s Critical Information Infrastructure
results.summary: The Minister stated that mandatory, centralized government security vetting for all personnel accessing Singapore's critical information infrastructure (CIIs) will not be introduced as a standalone measure. Instead, the Cybersecurity Act requires CII owners to implement access management controls, monitor for anomalies, and investigate unauthorized activities to mitigate insider threats. The Government emphasizes a "zero trust" architecture where access controls grant least privileged rights and continuous verification is prioritized over upstream vetting alone. While specific vetting requirements are not publicly disclosed to prevent them from becoming easy targets for adversaries, the Minister confirmed that relevant security measures exist for certain access types without detailing the full scope of current employer-led processes.

results.title: Measures to Encourage Prudence Among Borrowers as Household Liabilities Outpaced Asset Growth
results.summary: Over the full year of 2025, Household Assets grew faster than liabilities, with net worth continuing to expand as assets remain nine times the size of liabilities. Liquid assets exceed total liabilities, indicating strong balance sheets despite a quarterly rise in mortgages and personal loans driven by property transactions and investment needs. The Monetary Authority of Singapore maintains safeguards to pre-empt over-borrowing, including a Total Debt Servicing Ratio with an interest rate floor for affordability and strict minimum income requirements for unsecured credit. MAS also enforces borrowing limits, credit suspension for persistent debt, and public financial education via MoneySense to discourage excessive leverage. The agency will monitor developments and review policies as appropriate without specifying immediate new actions.

results.title: Measures to Ensure Accessibility of Creative Workspaces for Tenant Artists, Including Those at Goodman Arts Centre
results.summary: The National Arts Council (NAC) manages affordable creative workspaces at Goodman Arts Centre, comprising 26 Project Studios for short-term tenancies up to 12 months and 51 units under the Framework for Arts Spaces scheme supporting groups for up to nine years with up to 80% subvention. While the operator, Arts House Group, previously absorbed rising operational costs since the 2012 rate review, it has deferred rent increases scheduled for 2026 to support tenants amid economic uncertainty linked to the Middle East conflict. NAC and the operator will review rates again at the end of this year for 2027. Regarding other government-owned centres like Aliwal Art Centre and Stamford Art Centre, a broader study on improving arts spaces provision was announced last year and is currently ongoing, with findings to be shared when ready. The total tracked arts gross floor area in Singapore stands at 94,000 square metres, a 42% increase since 2010.

results.title: Monitoring Teaching Workload and Classroom Complexities in ITE to Ensure Effective Teaching and Sustainable Workloads
results.summary: The Institute of Technical Education (ITE) monitors educator workload via staff surveys, engagement sessions, and teaching hour indicators, while tracking student numbers with Special Educational Needs (SEN) to guide resource planning. Support structures include deployed mentors for classroom management guidance and specialised staff such as SEN support officers, socio-emotional counsellors, and student welfare officers who provide direct student assistance and educator training on diverse needs. All ITE educators receive mandatory training on recognising SEN conditions and implementing effective teaching strategies, with additional modules covering classroom management, student engagement, and behavioural regulation. To reduce administrative burdens, ITE adopts digital solutions like the internship management portal that automates workflows. Staff well-being is further supported through flexible work arrangements, fitness programmes, and free health checks, ensuring sustainable workloads amidst diverse classroom complexities.

results.title: Monitoring of Student Food Consumption Under Healthy Meals in Schools Programme and Plans to Diversify Menu Options
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the monitoring of student food consumption under the Healthy Meals in Schools Programme (HMSP) and plans for diversifying menu options or implementing student taste-testing feedback loops have already been addressed. This matter was previously covered in the Ministry of Education's written responses to Oral Parliamentary Questions Nos 72, 73, and 74 on 8 April 2026. No new details, timelines, or specific operational constraints regarding these initiatives were provided during this session.

results.title: Low-altitude Economy: Building the Flying Car, Air Tourism and Drone Logistics Industry
results.summary: Singapore will adopt the "low-altitude economy" terminology to guide its strategy, prioritizing regional air mobility and air tourism over domestic eVTOL transport due to congested airspace and limited marginal utility. While extensive domestic drone logistics face constraints, the government supports mature drone technologies through research funding, innovation trials, and regulatory sandboxes. The Ministry of Transport will consult stakeholders to develop a national strategy focusing on cross-border travel to adjacent offshore islands and cargo transfer with neighbouring countries, rather than extensive ground-replacement services. This approach balances safety and urban constraints while fostering a triple-helix ecosystem involving government, industry, and research institutes to build a viable regional aviation sector.

results.title: National Security Assessment of Proposed Simba-M1 Merger
results.summary: The proposed Simba-M1 merger is currently under review by the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) under the Telecom and Media Competition Code. The assessment evaluates commitments regarding public interest, national security, and competition levels before a final decision is made. While specific critical infrastructure systems remain undisclosed for national security reasons, telcos must adhere to strict security and resilience requirements to safeguard critical telecommunications infrastructure. The Government will not disclose designated critical systems but confirms that operators play a vital role in safeguarding the network through these adherence measures.

results.title: New Solutions Deployed to Provide Same-floor Lift Access in HDB Blocks
results.summary: The Minister stated that while the Lift Upgrading Programme has successfully upgraded the vast majority of HDB blocks, approximately 100 blocks, including the four at Hougang Central, remain unfeasible due to high costs or site constraints. HDB continuously monitors new technical solutions to address these remaining cases but has not yet deployed specific methods for Blocks 830 to 832 and 835 Hougang Central. For residents with urgent medical or mobility needs, the Lift Access Housing Grant of up to $80,000 is available to assist in purchasing a flat with direct lift access. Residents requiring assistance with this application should approach HDB directly.

results.title: Number of Variable Capital Companies in Singapore
results.summary: As of 1 April 2026, Singapore hosts 1,338 Variable Capital Companies (VCCs) managed by licensed fund managers and banks. The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) conducts regular monitoring, requiring fund managers to remediate control weaknesses identified during reviews. In the preceding year, 25 VCCs were found holding no assets without valid reason; their managers were directed to de-register these entities. MAS may also publish good practices for industry-wide adoption. While the Minister noted that some VCCs lack assets or investors, specific counts for entities with zero investors were not provided in the response.

results.title: Police Hotline Calls for Outrage of Modesty Cases in 2025 and Investigation Outcomes
results.summary: The Police do not track the volume of hotline calls reporting outrage of modesty but recorded 1,531 reported cases in 2025. All reported cases were investigated, though some remain under investigation or prosecution. Consequently, the proportion of calls resulting in investigations or convictions cannot be calculated as call data is unavailable. The Minister clarified that while all reported cases receive investigation, the specific conversion rate from hotline calls to outcomes is not tracked.

results.title: Police Reports and Investigations on Pro-Palestine and Pro-Israel Activities Since October 2023, and Investigation Outcomes
results.summary: Since October 2023, the Police investigated eight cases of unlawful public assemblies related to the Israel-Hamas conflict, all supporting Palestine. Investigations were initiated due to potential offences under the Public Order Act 2009, not the causes supported. Currently, four cases are under investigation, one defendant was acquitted pending an appeal by the Attorney-General's Chambers, and three concluded with police warnings. No investigations were reported for pro-Israel causes during this period.

results.title: Prevalence of Seniors Living Alone with Severe Lower Limb Mobility Loss or Bilateral Amputation
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health stated that the Ministry of Health does not track the specific number of seniors living alone with severe lower limb mobility loss or bilateral amputation. Instead, eligibility for existing support schemes is determined by an applicant's functional ability to perform Activities of Daily Living rather than the specific type of disability. Current measures include the enhanced Home Personal Care scheme, the Enhancement for Active Seniors (EASE) programme offering up to 95% subsidies for home modifications, and the Seniors' Mobility and Enabling Fund (SMF) providing up to 90% subsidies for assistive devices. The Home Caregiving Grant offers up to $600 monthly for seniors with moderate disabilities. Applications for EASE and SMF can be made through public healthcare institutions, community service providers, or directly via the Housing and Development Board and the Agency for Integrated Care.

results.title: Prevalence of Use of Local SIM Cards for Scams following Enactment of Law Enforcement and Other Matters Act
results.summary: Following the enactment of the Law Enforcement and Other Matters Bill on 2 April 2024, scams involving phone calls and SMSes decreased by 19% and 65% respectively between 2024 and 2025. To further deter offences, discretionary caning of up to 12 strokes and a ban on subscribing to new mobile lines will apply to scam mules providing SIM cards from 30 December 2025. In 2025, agencies disrupted over 105,000 scam-related mobile lines. Effective 28 February 2026, the maximum number of postpaid SIM cards a person can register across all telcos is capped at 10. Additionally, a common prefix for Government agency calls is being implemented to help citizens identify official communications. The Government retains the option to adjust the postpaid SIM card limit if the scam situation warrants it.

results.title: Proportion of Inmates with Dependent Children Below Age of 18 and Tracking Outcomes of Children of Incarcerated Parents
results.summary: As of 31 December 2024, 15.4% of inmates under the Singapore Prison Service (SPS) custody have at least one dependent child below 18 years old. The Ministry does not actively track specific outcomes such as educational attainment or youth offending for these children. However, SPS proactively identifies inmates with children under 21 upon admission to assess family needs and refer them to community resources, provided the inmate consents. Initiatives like the Yellow Ribbon Community Project engage volunteers to support families and link them with social service agencies and Family Service Centres. A 2017 study noted that one in five incarcerated parents had a child who subsequently offended, often entering the criminal justice system at a younger age than their parents. These measures aim to strengthen protective factors and mitigate the risk of intergenerational offending.

results.title: Proportion of Total Subsidised Community Dialysis Capacity Currently Served by National Kidney Foundation
results.summary: The National Kidney Foundation currently serves 90% of subsidised community dialysis capacity, while the Kidney Dialysis Foundation accounts for the remaining 10%. The Ministry of Health acknowledges this concentration but states it does not constitute a systemic risk to patient access. The Ministry welcomes additional non-profit providers to diversify the ecosystem and will partner with them to strengthen the overall service landscape. Specific incentives or support mechanisms to reduce entry barriers for existing healthcare non-profits were not detailed in the response.

results.title: Proposal for Beverage Container Return Points to be Located Within Five-minute Walk of Residential Estates
results.summary: The Minister stated that at launch, over 1,000 Reverse Vending Machines (RVMs) will be located at accessible sites like HDB void decks, ensuring 90% of residents live within a five-minute walk. The number of return points will double to 2,000 within the first year. Locations for the additional 1,000 RVMs will be determined based on return patterns and community feedback. The scheme operator, BCRS Ltd., will consider the suggestion to locate points at petrol kiosks, though no specific timeline or confirmation for this addition was provided in the response.

results.title: Protections for Platform Workers Affected by Food Delivery Platform Company's Exit from Singapore
results.summary: The Minister clarified that platform operators and associations should continue bilateral discussions on worker information transparency, guided by industry-wide principles on fair earnings and structural change visibility announced by NTUC on 11 September 2025. In the specific case of the food delivery platform exit, the operator collaborated with Workforce Singapore (WSG), NTUC's Employment and Employability Institute, and relevant unions to provide job matching, career coaching, and outreach support. Affected workers can access WSG's SkillsFuture and Career Conversion Programmes for transitioning to full-time roles or different platforms, while low-wage workers may seek financial assistance from social services offices. The response noted that no additional statutory measures were introduced beyond existing schemes, and the Government relies on continued collaborative efforts among unions, associations, and agencies to manage disruptions without establishing a new structured framework.

results.title: Public Feedback on SMRT MirrorMoves Community Space at Marina South Pier and Expansion Plans to Other MRT Stations with Lower Ridership
results.summary: The Ministry of Transport and Land Transport Authority reported receiving no public feedback regarding the MirrorMoves community space at Marina South Pier MRT station since its January 2026 launch. Operators will continue providing spaces for community activities where operations remain safe and undisturbed under existing engagement programmes. The Ministry did not confirm specific plans to expand these spaces to other stations with lower ridership, noting that future decisions depend on operational safety and commuter flow constraints. No timeline or eligibility criteria for expansion were stated in the response.
