results.title: Public Health Indicators to be Published to Enable Public to Track Health Outcomes and System Pressures
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) currently publishes various public health indicators, including capacity metrics on its website, annual survey data on lifestyle risks and nutrition, and key performance indicators in the Budget Book covering life expectancy and disease prevalence. MOH stated that reporting health outcomes on a fortnightly or monthly basis is inappropriate because such metrics take time to evolve meaningfully. While the Member raised concerns about specific waiting time thresholds and the need for a public dashboard to track system pressures in an ageing society, MOH confirmed it is reviewing these measures to ensure tracked indicators provide meaningful insights that encourage correct behaviours. The Ministry assured that it will continue to consider feedback to determine the most effective way to present information for public scrutiny while maintaining a balance between data utility and operational reality.

results.title: Quantifying Lifetime Income and Retirement Adequacy Impact of Extended Caregiving Interruptions
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that the Government does not track the duration, frequency, or specific circumstances of employment breaks taken for caregiving, as these are considered personal family arrangements. Consequently, the Government is unable to quantify the impact of such interruptions on lifetime income or retirement adequacy, nor can it assess how these effects may accumulate across generations. No data or estimates regarding these specific financial impacts are currently available or published by the Ministry.

results.title: Rationale for Correction Direction Issued on 23 March 2026 for Correction Notice to Be Published in The Straits Times
results.summary: On 23 March 2026, the POFMA Office issued a correction direction requiring a notice in *The Straits Times* alongside online publications. This decision addressed the specific case of The Online Citizen (TOC), which has a documented history of disseminating falsehoods and was previously designated as a Declared Online Location in July 2023 and July 2025. Over the past six years, TOC and its affiliated pages have received 25 Correction Directions despite these designations. The Minister explained that supplementing online corrections with a print notice was necessary to mitigate the broad reach of TOC's content and ensure facts reach audiences beyond the digital environment. The Government will assess the need for print corrections on a case-by-case basis to ensure effectiveness.

results.title: Reasons for Attrition in Foster Parenting Scheme and Proposed Guidelines for Co-parenting Arrangements for Fostering
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) is currently developing digital access for foster parents to view foster children's education and healthcare information via HealthHub and Parents Gateway, addressing concerns about appointment cancellations and information gaps. While MSF does not assume reliance on Child LifeSG Credits for fostering allowances, it has strengthened support measures including increasing allowances for teenagers from October 2025 and removing minimum household income and educational requirements from July 2024. The average annual attrition rate for foster parents over the last three years is approximately 6%, driven by new family commitments, health or age-related factors, and relocation. MSF remains open to co-parenting arrangements between foster and biological parents where appropriate to facilitate reunification, with post-reunification monitoring involving monthly safety checks by case officers for at least 12 months.

results.title: Reasons for Declining Pass Rates in Classes 3 and 3A Practical Driving Tests
results.summary: The Traffic Police maintains unchanged, high competency standards for Classes 3 and 3A Practical Driving Tests, noting that passing rates below 50% at some centres reflect these rigorous benchmarks rather than a decline in standards. Responsibility for curriculum development and instruction quality lies with driving schools and private instructors, who are incentivised by the publication of their respective passing rates. The Ministry advises learners to ensure proficiency before attempting tests, attributing low pass rates to the gap between learner readiness and the fixed competency requirements set by the Traffic Police.

results.title: Regulation of Sale and Use of Smart Glasses with AI Capabilities
results.summary: Smart glasses with AI capabilities in Singapore are regulated under existing data protection and safety frameworks, primarily the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA). Organisations offering these products must ensure compliance regarding data collection, usage, disclosure, and obtaining proper consent. Any transfer of personal data to overseas third-party contractors requires maintaining protection standards comparable to Singapore's. Non-compliance may result in financial penalties under the PDPA, while non-consensual recording or misuse of private content constitutes criminal offences under the Penal Code and Protection from Harassment Act.

results.title: Reintroduction of Course Fees Relief from 2027 and Alternative Personal Income Tax Reliefs for Skills Upgrading
results.summary: Course Fees Relief has lapsed from Year of Assessment 2026, with no plans to reintroduce it from 2027 or introduce alternative personal income tax reliefs for skills upgrading. The Government cites existing targeted support measures as sufficient alternatives, including course fee subsidies, training allowances, salary support via Career Conversion Programmes, and SkillsFuture Credit for out-of-pocket costs. These instruments are designed to directly fund lifelong learning and upskilling rather than providing broad tax rebates. Consequently, the specific tax relief mechanisms requested by the Member will not be implemented.

results.title: Remote Work for Non-essential Services to Reduce Use of Transport amid Petrol Price Surges
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that the Government has not planned to encourage remote work for non-essential services specifically in response to petrol price surges linked to the Middle East situation. This position was confirmed during a parliamentary debate on 7 April 2026, following a Ministerial Statement that elaborated on the current fuel situation. Consequently, there is no temporary policy measure or operational constraint introduced to reduce transport usage through remote work arrangements at this time.

results.title: Removing Advertisements on Online Platforms for Illegal Subletting of HDB Shops' Outdoor Display Areas
results.summary: Town Councils are empowered under the Town Councils Act to regulate Outdoor Display Areas (ODAs) and may revoke or withhold permits if illegal subletting is suspected. Regarding online advertisements on platforms like Carousell and Facebook, Town Councils can report listings to platform operators for review and removal where terms of service are contravened or permitted activities are facilitated. The Minister clarified that revoking ODA permits serves as an enforcement mechanism but did not specify whether this action effectively deters illegal subletting. Assistance for Town Councils in monitoring these platforms was not detailed beyond the existing reporting framework.

results.title: Remuneration, Allowance or Benefits of Grassroots Advisers Who Are Not Elected MPs
results.summary: Grassroots Advisers (GRAs) are not classified as officers in the Public Service and receive no remuneration, allowances, or benefits for their roles. While GRAs and grassroots organisations access specific information and data necessary to discharge their functions effectively, this sharing occurs strictly in accordance with relevant legislation and prevailing public service regulations. The Minister clarified that such information is provided only to the extent required for effective work execution, distinguishing it from general public data. No other financial incentives or special access privileges beyond these regulatory constraints were confirmed for non-elected GRAs.

results.title: Research on Correlation of PSLE-based School Placement and Socio-economic Status Performance Gap
results.summary: The Ministry of Education confirmed it has not assessed whether PSLE-based school placement amplifies the socio-economic status (SES) performance gap and does not plan to do so at this time, citing multiple influencing factors. Instead, MOE focuses on providing support to disadvantaged students through initiatives like Full Subject-Based Banding and targeted academic interventions in reading, literacy, and numeracy. While the Ministry will continue monitoring SES trends and has reviewed a proposal for a tie-breaker mechanism for lower-SES students in over-subscribed schools, no specific timeline or outcome for this review was stated. The Minister also noted that while teacher working hours are high, schools still provide various learning support programmes to help students strengthen foundational skills.

results.title: Resources for Hoarding Management Core Group, New Environment Action Team, Town Councils and Partners in Addressing Active Hoarding Cases
results.summary: As of June 2025, the Hoarding Management Core Group and New Environment Action Team (NEAT) have decluttered 308 severe cases while addressing 450 active cases, an increase from 253 in December 2021. The Minister attributed this rise to greater public awareness following NEAT's January 2025 launch and improved early identification efforts. The strategy pools resources across public, private, and people sectors to coordinate volunteers and experts for sustainable management. An ageing population may further increase case numbers, necessitating continued resource optimisation through this whole-of-society approach.

results.title: Response to Decline in Percentage of Graduates Securing Full-time Employment in 2025 Graduate Employment Survey
results.summary: The Ministry of Education stated that the decline in full-time graduate employment rates from 79.4% in 2024 to 74.4% in 2025 has already been addressed in responses to Oral Parliamentary Questions Nos 105 and 106 on 7 April 2026. No new measures were introduced or considered during this specific session as the matter was previously handled. Consequently, no additional policy actions, timelines, or eligibility rules were announced in this debate.

results.title: Retention Rates of Nursing Graduates from Local IHLs and Adjusting Nursing School Intake for 2030 Healthcare Workforce Targets
results.summary: Approximately 80% of local IHL nursing graduates enter the profession annually, with 70% remaining after five years. Nursing student intakes rose from 2,100 in 2022 to nearly 2,300 in 2025, though further increases are constrained by diverse career interests and existing cohort allocations. To meet the 2030 target of 82,000 healthcare workers, the government will focus on improving retention, recruiting mid-career entrants via skills conversion, and augmenting with foreign nurses. Local nurses will remain the core and majority of the workforce while these measures ensure long-term manpower needs are met.

results.title: Review Frequency of Social Support Scheme Adequacy and Framework to Trigger Temporary Support Schemes
results.summary: The Government regularly reviews social support schemes to ensure responsiveness to household needs, monitoring key indicators such as real income growth alongside outcomes in employment, housing, health, and education. These metrics allow the Government to assess support efficacy, identify unmet needs, and determine if temporary assistance measures are required to cushion exceptional shocks. No specific economic thresholds or predetermined triggers for out-of-cycle reviews were stated; instead, the Government assesses each situation holistically, considering the scale and nature of disruptions alongside existing support adequacy before deploying temporary measures.

results.title: Review of Early Intervention Programmes for Infants and Children to Include Subsidies for Standalone Therapies
results.summary: The Minister clarified that access to Government-funded Early Intervention Programme for Infants and Children (EIPIC) or EIPIC-P requires a formal paediatrician assessment to determine support levels before referral. Children under six receive individualised education plans developed by professionals in consultation with families. For standalone therapies like behavioural or speech therapy outside these funded programmes, parents can utilise the Child Development Account to pay for services registered with the Early Childhood Development Agency. Special education schools are resourced with Allied Health Professionals, including occupational, physio, and speech therapists, who provide in-school support alongside teachers to integrate therapy into daily learning. The Ministry of Education also partners with agencies to offer roving school-based educational support services for students with sensory and physical impairments joining mainstream schools.

results.title: Review of Mental Capacity Act 2008 and Professional Deputies and Donees Scheme to Enhance Availability and Accessibility of Professional Donee Options
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development confirmed that the review of the Mental Capacity Act 2008 and the Professional Deputies and Donees Scheme will include enhancing the availability and accessibility of professional donee options. This enhancement specifically targets expanding the pool of eligible professionals and institutional donees. The response directly addresses the query regarding the scope of the ongoing legislative and scheme review without providing further timelines or specific implementation details.

results.title: Review of Minimum Staff-to-child Ratios for Infant Care and Preschool Class Level
results.summary: The Minister clarified that minimum staff-to-child ratios for preschools were formally reviewed upon the enactment of the Early Childhood Development Centres Act in 2017 to ensure safety and well-being. While legislated ratios set minimum safety standards, preschools retain operational flexibility to exceed these requirements based on programming objectives and manpower availability. To support children with developmental needs in inclusive settings, preschools must obtain Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) approval if enrolling such children exceeds 15% of total capacity to ensure sufficient resources. Additionally, institutions may deploy extra staff or engage Early Intervention professionals to support these classes. The review considered international benchmarks and sector engagement to address developmental needs across different age groups.

results.title: Reviewing Income Eligibility Thresholds for Financial Assistance Schemes given Cost-of-Living Pressures
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) reviews ComCare income benchmarks regularly, noting the current $800 gross monthly per capita threshold is indicative rather than a strict qualifying criterion. Families exceeding this limit with valid needs may still receive assistance on a case-by-case basis. ComCare assistance rates are adjusted to account for projected inflation, and the threshold remains within a buffer following a review a year ago. Additional support includes enhanced Cost-of-Living Special Payments of up to $400 to $600 in September 2026 and CDC vouchers of $500 brought forward to June 2026. MSF monitors economic impacts on low-income families and is prepared to make further adjustments as needed.

results.title: Reviewing Mitochondrial Replacement for Inclusion as Option for Couples Seeking Assisted Reproductive Treatments
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) acknowledges mitochondrial replacement, a novel "three-person" IVF technique using donor mitochondria to prevent severe mitochondrial disease transmission, as an emerging approach. MOH will study evidence from clinical trials and consider ethical implications while monitoring international research outcomes. The Ministry has not yet committed to including this procedure as an option for couples seeking assisted reproductive technology treatments. A formal review or inclusion decision remains pending based on future evidence and ethical assessments.

results.title: Revision of Lease Conditions for Essential Service Businesses in HDB and Private-owned Shops
results.summary: The Minister clarified that HDB prioritizes comprehensive town planning and strategic space allocation over revising lease conditions to support essential service businesses. To foster affordable healthcare, HDB collaborates with the Ministry of Health to allocate rental shops for clinics and has piloted price-quality tenders for private general practitioners. For social enterprises and inclusive businesses, direct allocation and subsidised rental spaces are provided under the social enterprise policy, while socio-communal facilities like childcare and senior care centres are integrated into estate plans. Regarding privately-owned shops, usage and leasing conditions remain determined through market-based negotiations between businesses and owners, with the Government focusing on incentivising private developers via the Urban Redevelopment Authority's Community/Sports Facilities Scheme to co-locate non-profit community and sports users.

results.title: Safeguards to Prevent Disposable Carrier Bag Charges from Becoming Profit-Generating Mechanisms
results.summary: The National Environment Agency does not mandate specific uses for proceeds from the disposable carrier bag charge to avoid disadvantaging supermarket operators, though the charge aims to nudge consumers toward reducing bag usage. Supermarket operators obligated to implement the charge must annually publish data on bags supplied, proceeds collected, and their usage to ensure transparency and public accountability. While the majority of proceeds have been channeled toward environmental or social causes, including initiatives for lower-income families, the Government will continue monitoring operator usage and encouraging contributions to sustainability projects. The Ministry has not explicitly assessed the risk of the charge becoming a profit-generating mechanism but relies on annual reporting and ongoing monitoring as safeguards.

results.title: Safety Assessment of Hidden Car Door Handles and Regulatory Measures for Door Handle Safety Standards
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that all vehicles registered in Singapore must comply with internationally recognised safety standards, including UNECE Regulations on door latches and hinges. The Land Transport Authority (LTA) is actively monitoring ongoing regulatory reviews regarding door operability during emergencies at the UNECE, as well as similar reviews in markets such as China, Europe, and the United States. LTA stated it will collaborate closely with the industry when introducing any new safety requirements to enhance safety while minimising disruptions to road users. Specific data on related incidents or a definitive timeline for new regulatory measures were not provided in the response.

results.title: Short-term Subsidy Assistance for Rising Operational Costs due to Goodman Arts Centre's Rental Hikes
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated that the recent rent hikes at Goodman Arts Centre were addressed in a reply to Question 19 on 8 April 2026. The Minister did not provide specific details regarding funding decreases, subsidy reductions, or the reasons for the cost increases within this transcript. Consequently, no short-term subsidy assistance measures or operational constraints were introduced or confirmed in response to the inquiry about rising costs for the arts community. Further information on the rationale for the hikes or potential government support remains untracked in this specific exchange.

results.title: Singapore's Economic, Social and Cultural Status' Slope Ranking of Second out of 79 in PISA
results.summary: The Minister of State for Education clarified that Singapore's steep ESCS slope in PISA reflects a wider performance gap between high- and low-SES students rather than a lack of opportunity for the former. She affirmed that the Ministry will not cap the potential of high-performing students but remains committed to uplifting those from lower-income households through targeted support and after-school programmes. The Minister confirmed that MOE will continue to monitor this trend as part of its suite of overall educational outcome indicators to assess the effectiveness of current measures. While a Member raised concerns regarding early tracking and cited research linking it to increased SES inequality, the Minister stated she would review such research after the session.

results.title: Study on Potential Fire Hazards from Discarded Lithium-ion Batteries and Power Banks
results.summary: Improper disposal of lithium-ion batteries and power banks in the municipal waste stream poses fire risks, prompting the National Environment Agency (NEA) to collaborate with the Singapore Civil Defence Force and Town Councils on preventive measures. Since July 2025, NEA has intensified public education efforts, distributing handling posters to Town Councils and advising the public to tape battery ends or seal leaks before recycling via the ALBA E-Waste Smart Recycling scheme. High-risk items, including damaged or high-capacity batteries from electric mobility devices, must be recycled through ALBA's collection drives, depot drop-offs, or doorstep services. The public is reminded to use designated e-waste bins for standard items while NEA continues monitoring fire incidents to assess the necessity of further safeguards.

results.title: Subsidies for Private Speech and Occupational Therapy Sessions for Autistic Children
results.summary: The Minister stated that the Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) funds EIPIC and EIPIC-P with means-tested fee caps, where median-income families pay up to $210 and $360 monthly respectively. While ECDA recommends using registered providers to regulate costs, parents may access supplementary services via the Child Development Account (CDA). The Minister declined to introduce a tiered co-payment model for private providers or expand CDA usage beyond ECDA-registered early intervention services, noting the current provider list is comprehensive. Families requiring additional therapy beyond the standard five to 12 weekly hours are directed to consult professionals on their Individualised Education Plan or utilize CDA funds for registered providers.

results.title: Subsidised Co-living Options for Singles, Addressing Motherhood Penalty, Publicising Shared Parental Leave Take-up Rates as Marriage and Parenthood Ideas
results.summary: The Government announced a Workgroup on 26 February 2026 to drive a society-wide reset on marriage and parenthood, with composition and terms of reference to be released soon. While specific deliverables and timelines remain under fine-tuning, the Workgroup will explore options for social interaction, including subsidised co-living and community programming for singles, alongside measures to support working parents. The Workplace Fairness Act, prohibiting adverse employment decisions based on pregnancy or caregiving, is targeted for implementation by end-2027. The Government will also consider mandating companies to publicly disclose parental leave policies and utilisation rates to promote transparency, while exploring incentives for work-life friendly practices without micro-managing sector-specific operational demands.

results.title: Supporting Commercial Viability and Development of Local Farms Through Export Market Access While Maintaining Domestic Food Supply Resilience
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency (SFA) supports local farms accessing premium export markets by providing a library of overseas import requirements on its website and leveraging Singapore's food safety recognition. In 2024, protocols with China enabled the export of six locally farmed fish species to that market. SFA collaborates with agencies like the Singapore Agro-Food Enterprises Federation and international partners to further enhance market access. Businesses may utilize Enterprise Singapore schemes, including the Market Readiness Assistance Grant for overseas promotion and the Enterprise Development Grant for business upgrades, to improve commercial viability. These efforts aim to increase demand offtake while maintaining domestic supply commitments.

results.title: Supporting Educators in Vocational Institutions to Integrate AI into Teaching and Learning
results.summary: The Ministry of Education supports vocational educators in integrating artificial intelligence through mandatory AI competency training for new lecturers and optional Continuing Professional Development programmes for in-service staff. All polytechnics and the Institute of Technical Education provide dedicated AI tools to assist with lesson design, learning analytics, and customised student feedback. Lecturers further develop domain-specific skills via industry collaborations, including attachments and consultancy projects, with insights directly incorporated into curricula. Effectiveness is evaluated through regular reviews of CPD programmes using pre- and post-training assessments and lecturer feedback. Sector-wide communities of practice facilitate best practice sharing and cross-institutional experimentation. Additionally, a new Committee for AI in Higher Education, chaired by the Minister for Education, has been established to provide strategic direction on key AI priorities across all Institutes of Higher Learning.

results.title: Take-up Rate of Graduate Diploma in Mental Health
results.summary: The Graduate Diploma in Mental Health, jointly offered by the Institute of Mental Health and National University of Singapore, has an average annual intake of approximately 25 medical professionals. This figure has gradually increased since the programme began. The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health noted that while the current scale supports family physicians in diagnosing and managing community mental health conditions, the intake may be expanded if national needs increase. No specific take-up rate percentage or fixed timeline for scaling up was provided in the response.

results.title: Targeted Measures to Ease Short-term Financial Strain on Heavily Leveraged Homeowners
results.summary: The Minister stated that the Monetary Authority of Singapore and HDB maintain safeguards to pre-empt over-borrowing and strengthen borrower resilience against rising interest rates. The Total Debt Servicing Ratio calculation incorporates an interest rate floor for both private and HDB loans, while HDB offers concessionary loans pegged to CPF Ordinary Account rates. Although interest rates have remained stable domestically, the Minister noted global uncertainty and urged home buyers to exercise prudence in their purchasing decisions. No new targeted measures were introduced during this session.

results.title: Targeted Support for Platform Drivers to Mitigate Rising Operating Costs
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport directed that the question regarding targeted support for platform and taxi drivers to offset rising operating costs was addressed in Ministerial Statements delivered on 7 April 2026. These statements, covering the impact of the Middle East situation, were issued by the Deputy Prime Minister, Minister for Trade and Industry, Acting Minister for Transport, Senior Minister of State for Finance, and Coordinating Minister for National Security. The transcript does not specify the exact measures, timelines, or eligibility criteria for any such support within these statements.

results.title: Terms for Infrastructure Upgrades and Range of Electrical Capacity in EV Charging Contracts in HDB Car Parks
results.summary: Tenders for EV charger deployment at HDB carparks are separate from electrical infrastructure upgrades due to differing scopes and contractors. While a tender for upgrading 300 multi-storey carparks was awarded in May 2025, electrical capacity varies across sites. Currently, HDB carparks typically support three to 12 slow charging points based on existing capacity. The specific maximum number of installable chargers before requiring further upgrades depends on the individual carpark's electrical capacity, which is not uniformly defined.

results.title: Town Councils' Community Improvement Projects Accepted for FY2025
results.summary: The Ministry of National Development has suspended the Community Improvement Projects Committee (CIPC) nomination process for both FY2025 and FY2026 cycles. This decision allows Town Councils sufficient time to complete FY2024 projects, which were awarded in January 2026 following an extension of the submission deadline due to post-General Election 2025 handover processes. No specific start date for the FY2025 cycle has been set, and Town Councils will be informed of the timeline once the exercise is ready to commence. Consequently, no FY2025 nominations have been accepted or are currently open for submission.

results.title: Traffic Offences Issued in 1Q2026 Following Step-up Enforcements and Breakdown of Traffic Offences by Type
results.summary: Between January and February 2026, 55,044 traffic violations were detected, representing a 6% increase from the 51,944 recorded in the same period of 2025. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs noted that statistics for March 2026 are not yet available. The top three violation types for the first two months of both years are detailed in Table 1 of the official response. Specific breakdown figures for March remain pending until further data is released by the Traffic Police.

results.title: Training for Student Leaders to Recognise Early Warning Signs of Suicidal Ideation or Severe Distress Among Students
results.summary: Through Character and Citizenship Education, all students learn to care for one another and seek help from trusted adults. Schools provide specific training to Peer Support Leaders (PSLs) to offer age-appropriate comfort, encourage help-seeking, and employ active listening or positive coping strategies. Older PSLs receive additional instruction in empathy and coping strategies. Crucially, PSLs are not expected to identify suicidal ideation or provide peer counselling. Instead, they are trained to alert a trusted adult regarding concerns they cannot resolve. Teachers regularly meet with PSLs to support their capacity development and well-being.

results.title: Twinning Programmes between Autonomous Universities and Chinese Universities for Admission into PhD Programmes
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that some autonomous universities (AUs) have established joint PhD programmes with Chinese universities. These specific programmes are open to students from both institutions regardless of nationality, facilitating mutual academic exchange. The Minister noted that AUs generally collaborate with a wide range of international partners for education and research, though no specific list of twinning arrangements or reciprocal eligibility rules for Singaporeans pursuing PhDs in China was provided. Further details on official or informal twinning programmes were not explicitly detailed in the response.

results.title: Unchanged Cap on CPF Balances Earning Additional 1% Interest
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the $60,000 cap on CPF balances earning an additional 1% interest, unchanged since 2008, is not linked to Full Retirement Sum (FRS) growth because retirement sums are set independently based on required retirement payouts. Consequently, the Government stated there is no interest forgone by members due to this cap. While the 1% extra interest applies to the first $60,000 for all members and an additional 1% on the first $30,000 for those aged 55 and above, the Minister noted that retirement sums guide savings needs rather than dictate the interest cap. The Government complements these measures with initiatives like the Majulah Package, Silver Support Scheme, and a new CPF Top-Up for older Singaporeans announced in Budget 2026 to help build retirement savings.

results.title: Validity Checks, Automated Reminders and Tracking of Repeat Offences to Ensure Doctors and Dentists Practise with Valid Credentials
results.summary: Between 1 January 2023 and 31 December 2025, three practitioners aged 65 and above were investigated for practising without valid credentials; none were foreigners or repeat offenders. The Secretariat of healthcare Professional Boards (SPB) informs all registered practitioners of their Practising Certificate validity via electronic copies and sends regular reminders at least two months before renewal. Upon expiry, SPB requires practitioners to cease clinical practice until a new certificate is issued. To support senior solo practitioners, SPB accommodates requests to direct communications to family members assisting with administrative matters for certificate renewal notifications.

results.title: Utilisation Rates of Parent Care Leave by Public Servants from 2023
results.summary: From 1 January 2025, the Civil Service expanded Parent Care Leave to Family Care Leave, covering grandparents, siblings, and grandchildren. Between 2023 and 2025, approximately 42,300 officers (46% of eligible staff) took at least one day of leave annually. The average duration was 1.74 days, with a median of two days. This data reflects usage trends for immediate family members under the updated leave framework.

results.title: Update on Efforts of Mercury Task Force to Tackle Heatwaves
results.summary: The Mercury Task Force coordinates a multi-agency heatwave response plan activated upon Meteorological Service Singapore forecasts. Measures include islandwide cooling centres, public advisories, and targeted support for high-risk groups like outdoor workers. Agencies refresh protocols annually from March to May to ensure stakeholder readiness. A heatwave is officially declared when the daily maximum temperature averages at least 35°C across all stations for three consecutive days, with a mean temperature of at least 29°C. The Minister stated this definition remains relevant to Singapore's current climate. No review of the declaration criteria was indicated in the response.

results.title: Workload with 2% to 9% Salary Increase for Teachers
results.summary: The Ministry of Education confirmed that the planned teacher salary revision of 2% to 9% is not tied to additional duties. This measure aims to maintain market competitiveness for the teaching corps without increasing workload. The salary review is part of periodic assessments to ensure the overall package remains competitive. No new operational constraints or eligibility conditions were stated regarding this specific salary adjustment.

results.title: Water Loss Rate in 2025 in National Water Distribution System
results.summary: In 2025, Singapore achieved a potable water loss rate of 7.2% within the national distribution system, ranking among the lowest globally. Six reported pipe damage cases occurred near land transport construction sites that year. Since 2023, the Public Utilities Board (PUB) has mandated GPS tagging for specific machinery to monitor high-risk works adjacent to critical pipelines, aiming to reduce damage risks and enhance network resilience. The Minister noted these figures are published in PUB's Annual and Sustainability Report.

results.title: Veterinary Practice Bill
results.summary: The Veterinary Practice Bill establishes the Veterinary Council to replace the Animals and Birds Act framework, aiming to raise professional standards, ensure public trust, and regulate allied animal-care providers. The new body will register veterinarians, including existing AVS-licensed practitioners granted a transition grace period, while introducing stricter penalties for unauthorised practice and a robust disciplinary system aligned with human healthcare. Speakers emphasised that affordability, cost transparency, and talent retention must be central objectives, calling for local degree pathways, subsidies, and clear guidelines on pricing and restricted practice scopes. The Bill also incorporates safeguards against frivolous complaints, defines the Council's composition with layperson representation, and sets out mechanisms for independent investigation and consumer protection measures.
